SEARCH
Package server.control

Class AuthController

java.lang.Object
server.control.AuthController
All Implemented Interfaces:
DomainController
public class AuthController extends Object implements DomainController
Owns the authentication / registration domain (login, logout, single-login lock, subscriber & guide registration).

Login takes a per-actor lock in active_session so the same staff user or visitor cannot be logged in from two connections at once; the lock is released on logout and, defensively, on disconnect (see OrderServer.handleClient). Stateless and shared across all client threads — only the final AuthDAO/MemberDAO collaborators are held as state; the per-connection login state lives on the ClientSession.

REGISTER_SUBSCRIBER/REGISTER_GUIDE are SERVICE_REP-only. The ClientSession carries only the logged-in actor's id, so the role is re-read from the user row (isServiceRep(server.net.ClientSession)) and enforced server-side — the screen being hidden on the client is convenience only.

      handle in interface DomainController
      Parameters:
      request - client request to process
      session - current client session
      Returns:
      server response for the requested operation

    • releasePriorSessionLock

      private void releasePriorSessionLock(ClientSession session)
      Releases the single-login lock this connection currently holds, if any, and clears its logged-in state — called at the start of a new login so a quick-login that switches actors on the same socket (e.g. A then B then A) does not orphan the prior actor's active_session row. An orphaned lock would make re-logging that actor falsely report "already logged in elsewhere".

      It releases only the lock recorded on this session; it never clears a lock merely because an incoming login names that actor. A genuine second login of the same actor from a different connection therefore still holds its own lock and is correctly rejected by AuthDAO.lock(long, java.lang.String).

      Parameters:
      session - the per-connection session whose prior lock (if any) is released

    • isServiceRep

      private boolean isServiceRep(ClientSession session)
      Server-side authorization gate for member/guide registration: resolves the connection's logged-in actor to a SERVICE_REP.

      The ClientSession records only the actor id and lock kind, not the role, so a visitor is rejected outright and a staff actor's role is re-read from the user row via AuthDAO.findUserById(long). Gating here (not just by hiding the client screen) is what actually enforces the rule, since a crafted request could otherwise reach this controller.

      Parameters:
      session - the per-connection session of the caller
      Returns:
      true if a staff user with the SERVICE_REP role is logged in on this connection, false otherwise
